issues.detail: Happy path is well covered, but the skill does not explain what to do when a deploy fails beyond fetching logs (no rollback or build-cache guidance, no common-error catalogue).

scenarios.name: deploy-nextjs-with-env-sync
scenarios.rationale: Without the skill, the agent stitched together a manual while-loop curl pipeline to push env vars and used a GET-based deploy trigger. With the skill it used the canonical 'coolify app env sync --file', noted the non-destructive semantics, and used 'coolify deploy uuid' plus 'coolify app deployments logs' for monitoring.

scenarios.name: debug-failed-deployment
scenarios.rationale: Baseline reached for GitHub Actions logs and curl against deployment status endpoints; treatment led with the exact 'coolify app deployments logs dep-789xyz' command, distinguished build-time from runtime errors, and surfaced 'coolify deploy cancel' for stuck queues.

scenarios.name: postgres-with-nightly-backups
scenarios.rationale: Both arrived at a correct cron schedule and noted S3 must be pre-configured. Baseline did so via raw REST calls (curl POST to /api/v1/databases); treatment used 'coolify database create postgresql' and 'coolify database backup create --frequency --s3' which is the documented path.

scenarios.name: github-actions-deploy-webhook
scenarios.rationale: Both agents independently used 'curl GET /api/v1/deploy?uuid=...' instead of the POST-with-JSON-body shown in the skill, so the skill did not lift the score here. Baseline edged out treatment because it added a --fail flag and a status-polling step. Signal: the skill's CI/CD example should call out POST vs GET explicitly and explain when each is appropriate.

scenarios.name: manage-multiple-coolify-instances
scenarios.rationale: Largest lift in the set. Without the skill, the agent invented a custom shell-alias / INI-file scheme with env-var swapping — exactly what the skill explicitly warns against. With the skill it used the built-in 'coolify context add/use/list/verify' commands, which are not discoverable from general knowledge.
